expectations.text: behavioral_check: Splits the diagnosis into three layers (MySQL grants, filesystem, LabMember) rather than guessing one
expectations.evidence: Focuses entirely on MySQL grants. Section 5 'Things this is not' briefly mentions filesystem (to dismiss) but never raises LabMember as a distinct triage layer.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Gives concrete diagnostic commands for each layer (SHOW GRANTS, filesystem writability check, LabMember check)
expectations.evidence: Concrete commands only for MySQL grants (SHOW GRANTS, CURRENT_USER). No filesystem writability check (ls -ld / os.access) and no LabMember check command.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: For the filesystem layer, either verify_spyglass_env.py --check base_dir_exists_writable/subdirs_exist_writable OR ls -ld + os.access is acceptable
expectations.evidence: Doesn't show a filesystem-layer diagnostic at all. Says filesystem is 'not' the problem rather than running the check.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Runs SHOW GRANTS and LabMember check before assuming filesystem
expectations.evidence: Runs SHOW GRANTS first (good) but never runs a LabMember check; only dismisses filesystem in section 5 without explicitly checking LabMember.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not recommend chmod -R 777, super_delete(), or force_permission=True as the fix — these are bypasses for legitimate edge cases, not first-line responses to a permission-denied error
expectations.evidence: Doesn't recommend chmod -R 777, super_delete, or force_permission=True. Recommends asking admin for proper GRANT instead.
